description: Fix the SDK base58 encoder and decoder so leading zero bytes round-trip correctly, preserve canonical outputs for known-good cases, and add focused regression coverage for direct codec behavior plus dependent identity helpers.

userStories.title: Confirm codec scope and regression inputs
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the implementation scope and regression inputs confirmed in the relevant SDK and test files so that the fix stays limited to the leading-zero codec bug.
acceptanceCriteria: Implementation work is limited to `packages/agent-world-sdk/src/identity.ts`, `packages/agent-world-sdk/src/peer-protocol.ts`, and affected test files needed for verification.
The regression inputs `[0]`, `[0,0]`, `[0,1]`, `[1]`, and `[1,0]` are explicitly represented in the resulting task breakdown or test plan.
userStories.notes: Confirmed scope for the leading-zero codec fix is limited to `packages/agent-world-sdk/src/identity.ts` for `base58Encode`, `packages/agent-world-sdk/src/peer-protocol.ts` for `base58Decode`/`multibaseToBase64`, and affected regression tests under `test/` that import built `dist/` artifacts. Existing broad identity coverage lives in `test/agentid-identity.test.mjs`, but the focused regression plan for this branch is a dedicated `test/base58.test.mjs` covering the explicit byte cases `[0]`, `[0,0]`, `[0,1]`, `[1]`, and `[1,0]` for encode, decode, and round-trip checks.

userStories.title: Identify deterministic identity helper fixtures
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a deterministic public-key fixture and fixed expected outputs identified for DID and multibase helpers so that regression tests assert stable literals instead of recomputing expectations.
acceptanceCriteria: A deterministic fixture is selected from existing repo coverage or introduced in tests for identity helper assertions.
Expected `deriveDidKey` and `toPublicKeyMultibase` outputs are recorded as explicit string literals in tests or implementation notes.
userStories.notes: Use a deterministic Ed25519 fixture generated from `nacl.sign.keyPair.fromSeed(Buffer.alloc(32, 1))`. The corresponding seed literal is `AQEBAQEBAQEBAQEBAQEBAQEBAQEBAQEBAQEBAQEBAQE=` and the derived public key literal is `iojj3XQJ8ZX9UtstPLpdcspnCb8dlBIb83SIAbQPb1w=`. Record these explicit expected helper outputs for later regression coverage: `deriveDidKey(pubB64) === "did:key:z6Mkon3Necd6NkkyfoGoHxid2znGc59LU3K7mubaRcFbLfLX"` and `toPublicKeyMultibase(pubB64) === "z6Mkon3Necd6NkkyfoGoHxid2znGc59LU3K7mubaRcFbLfLX"`.

userStories.title: Fix base58 encoding for leading zero bytes
userStories.description: As a developer, I need `base58Encode` to emit canonical leading `1` prefixes so that byte arrays with leading zeros preserve their exact binary value when encoded.
userStories.notes: Updated `packages/agent-world-sdk/src/identity.ts` so `base58Encode()` returns only the canonical leading `1` prefix sequence when the input is all zero bytes, instead of appending the synthetic zero digit. Verified the boundary cases `[0] -> "1"`, `[0,0] -> "11"`, `[0,1] -> "12"`, `[1] -> "2"`, and `[1,0] -> "5R"`, and confirmed the SDK TypeScript build still passes.

userStories.title: Fix base58 decoding for leading one prefixes
userStories.description: As a developer, I need `base58Decode` to reconstruct leading zero bytes from canonical base58 inputs so that identity-related values decode without corruption.
userStories.notes: Updated `packages/agent-world-sdk/src/peer-protocol.ts` so `base58Decode()` treats all-leading-`1` inputs as pure zero-byte payloads instead of preserving the synthetic accumulator byte. Verified the canonical boundary cases `"1" -> [0]`, `"11" -> [0,0]`, `"12" -> [0,1]`, `"2" -> [1]`, and `"5R" -> [1,0]`, then re-ran the SDK build plus the full repo build/test sequence successfully.

userStories.title: Add direct encode regression coverage
userStories.description: As a developer, I need focused encode assertions in a dedicated codec regression test file so that canonical leading-zero outputs are verified independently of broader protocol tests.
acceptanceCriteria: `test/base58.test.mjs` asserts exact encode results for `[0]`, `[0,0]`, `[0,1]`, `[1]`, and `[1,0]`.
The encode assertions import built artifacts rather than source files directly.
userStories.notes: Verified `test/base58.test.mjs` exists and asserts exact encode results for `[0]`, `[0,0]`, `[0,1]`, `[1]`, and `[1,0]` by importing `base58Encode` from the built SDK artifact at `packages/agent-world-sdk/dist/identity.js`. Re-ran `npm --prefix packages/agent-world-sdk run build` and `node --test test/base58.test.mjs`; both passed.

userStories.title: Add direct decode and round-trip regression coverage
userStories.description: As a developer, I need decode and round-trip assertions in the codec regression test file so that the leading-zero boundary cases are validated byte-for-byte in both directions.
acceptanceCriteria: `test/base58.test.mjs` asserts exact decode results for `"1"`, `"11"`, `"12"`, `"2"`, and `"5R"`.
Each round-trip assertion verifies byte-for-byte equality with the original input.
userStories.notes: Extended `test/base58.test.mjs` with exact decode assertions for `"1"`, `"11"`, `"12"`, `"2"`, and `"5R"`, plus byte-for-byte round-trip checks for `[0]`, `[0,0]`, `[0,1]`, `[1]`, and `[1,0]`. Exported `base58Decode` from `packages/agent-world-sdk/src/peer-protocol.ts` and the SDK index so the regression can import the compiled artifact at `packages/agent-world-sdk/dist/peer-protocol.js`. Verified with `npm --prefix packages/agent-world-sdk run build`, `npm run build`, and `node --test test/*.test.mjs`.

userStories.title: Add deriveDidKey regression coverage
userStories.description: As a developer, I need deterministic `deriveDidKey` coverage so that the codec fix cannot silently change canonical DID output for known-good inputs.
acceptanceCriteria: Tests assert `deriveDidKey` returns a fixed expected DID string for the chosen deterministic fixture.
The expected DID value is written as an explicit literal and is not computed by the function under test.
The regression coverage lives in `test/base58.test.mjs` or another dedicated identity-focused test file that runs under `node --test test/*.test.mjs`.
userStories.notes: Extended `test/base58.test.mjs` with a deterministic `deriveDidKey()` assertion that uses the fixed public-key fixture `iojj3XQJ8ZX9UtstPLpdcspnCb8dlBIb83SIAbQPb1w=` and the explicit expected DID literal `did:key:z6Mkon3Necd6NkkyfoGoHxid2znGc59LU3K7mubaRcFbLfLX`. Verified with `npm --prefix packages/agent-world-sdk run build`, `npm run build`, and `node --test test/*.test.mjs`.

userStories.title: Add toPublicKeyMultibase regression coverage
userStories.description: As a developer, I need deterministic `toPublicKeyMultibase` coverage so that canonical multibase output remains stable after the codec change.
acceptanceCriteria: Tests assert `toPublicKeyMultibase` returns a fixed expected multibase string for the chosen deterministic fixture.
The expected multibase value is written as an explicit literal and is not computed by the function under test.
The regression coverage runs under `node --test test/*.test.mjs`.
userStories.notes: Extended `test/base58.test.mjs` with a deterministic `toPublicKeyMultibase()` assertion that uses the fixed public-key fixture `iojj3XQJ8ZX9UtstPLpdcspnCb8dlBIb83SIAbQPb1w=` and the explicit expected multibase literal `z6Mkon3Necd6NkkyfoGoHxid2znGc59LU3K7mubaRcFbLfLX`. Verified with `npm --prefix packages/agent-world-sdk run build`, `npm run build`, and `node --test test/*.test.mjs`.

userStories.title: Verify SDK package build after codec changes
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the SDK package build to pass after the codec updates so that downstream tests import fresh built artifacts with the fix applied.
acceptanceCriteria: The build output includes the updated codec logic needed by tests.
userStories.notes: Ran `npm --prefix packages/agent-world-sdk run build` after the codec and regression-test changes; the SDK TypeScript build completed successfully and confirmed the updated compiled artifacts are available for downstream tests.

userStories.title: Verify root build and test suite
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the repo build and test workflow to pass with the new regression coverage so that the base58 fix is validated the same way local development and CI expect.
userStories.notes: Added `test/base58.test.mjs` with exact encode assertions for `[0]`, `[0,0]`, `[0,1]`, `[1]`, and `[1,0]`, importing `base58Encode` from the built SDK artifact at `packages/agent-world-sdk/dist/identity.js`. Exported `base58Encode` from `packages/agent-world-sdk/src/identity.ts` and the SDK index so the regression can exercise the compiled implementation directly.
